paragraphs.en: Last Saturday was my friend Lily's birthday. She had a big party at her house. Her mum made a beautiful chocolate cake with ten candles on top.
paragraphs.fr: Samedi dernier, c'etait l'anniversaire de mon amie Lily. Elle a fait une grande fete chez elle. Sa maman a fait un magnifique gateau au chocolat avec dix bougies dessus.

paragraphs.en: I went to the party with my brother. We gave Lily a present — a book about animals because she loves animals. She was very happy and said thank you many times.
paragraphs.fr: Je suis alle(e) a la fete avec mon frere. Nous avons offert un cadeau a Lily — un livre sur les animaux parce qu'elle adore les animaux. Elle etait tres contente et a dit merci plein de fois.

paragraphs.en: Many guests came to the party. We danced to music and played games in the garden. Tom sang 'Happy Birthday' very loudly and everyone laughed.
paragraphs.fr: Beaucoup d'invites sont venus a la fete. Nous avons danse sur de la musique et joue a des jeux dans le jardin. Tom a chante 'Joyeux anniversaire' tres fort et tout le monde a ri.

paragraphs.en: Then Lily blew out the candles on her cake. She made a wish but she did not tell us what it was. We all had a piece of cake. It was delicious!
paragraphs.fr: Ensuite, Lily a souffle les bougies sur son gateau. Elle a fait un voeu mais elle ne nous a pas dit ce que c'etait. Nous avons tous eu un morceau de gateau. C'etait delicieux !

paragraphs.en: Lily's dad put balloons everywhere — red, blue, yellow and green. At the end of the party, each guest took a balloon home. We had a great time!
paragraphs.fr: Le papa de Lily a mis des ballons partout — rouges, bleus, jaunes et verts. A la fin de la fete, chaque invite a pris un ballon pour rentrer a la maison. Nous avons passe un super moment !

vocabulary.definition_en: le jour chaque annee qui correspond a la date ou l'on est ne
vocabulary.example: Today is my birthday — I am ten years old!

vocabulary.definition_en: quelque chose que l'on offre a quelqu'un, surtout pour une occasion speciale
vocabulary.example: I gave her a present for her birthday.

vocabulary.definition_en: un baton de cire avec une meche a l'interieur que l'on fait bruler pour donner de la lumiere
vocabulary.example: There were ten candles on the birthday cake.

vocabulary.definition_en: un petit sac en caoutchouc colore que l'on remplit d'air pour la decoration
vocabulary.example: The room was full of colourful balloons.

vocabulary.definition_en: un aliment sucre fait avec de la farine, des oeufs, du beurre et du sucre, cuit au four
vocabulary.example: Mum made a chocolate cake for the party.

vocabulary.definition_en: passe de 'sing' — produire des sons musicaux avec sa voix
passe de 'dance' — bouger son corps en rythme avec la musique
vocabulary.example: We danced to music all afternoon.

vocabulary.definition_en: passe de 'invite' — demander a quelqu'un de venir a un evenement
vocabulary.example: Lily invited all her friends to the party.

vocabulary.definition_en: une personne qui est invitee a une fete ou un evenement
vocabulary.example: There were twenty guests at the party.

grammar_points.title: Simple past: irregular verbs (had, went, came, gave, sang)
grammar_points.explanation: Certains verbes anglais sont **irreguliers** au passe — ils ne suivent PAS la regle du -ed. Il faut les apprendre par coeur. Voici les plus courants : **have → had**, **go → went**, **come → came**, **give → gave**, **sing → sang**.
examples: She had a big party. (have → had) = Elle a fait une grande fete.
I went to the party. (go → went) = Je suis alle(e) a la fete.
Many guests came. (come → came) = Beaucoup d'invites sont venus.
We gave her a present. (give → gave) = Nous lui avons offert un cadeau.
Tom sang 'Happy Birthday'. (sing → sang) = Tom a chante 'Joyeux anniversaire'.

grammar_points.title: Past simple: negative and affirmative with irregular verbs
grammar_points.explanation: Pour les phrases negatives au passe, on utilise **did not (didn't) + la forme de base** du verbe (PAS la forme du passe). C'est la meme chose pour les verbes reguliers et irreguliers.
examples: She did not tell us. (NOT: She did not told us.) = Elle ne nous a pas dit.
I didn't go to school yesterday. = Je ne suis pas alle(e) a l'ecole hier.
They didn't sing. = Ils n'ont pas chante.
Note: In affirmative sentences, use the past form: She told us. I went. They sang.

expressions.example: Happy birthday, Lily! Here is your present.
expressions.note: La phrase standard pour souhaiter un joyeux anniversaire en anglais.

expressions.meaning_fr: J'ai recu un(e)... / J'ai eu un(e)...
expressions.example: I got a new bike for my birthday!
expressions.note: 'Got' est le passe de 'get'. Tres courant en anglais parle pour parler de choses que l'on a recues.

expressions.meaning_fr: Nous avons passe un super moment
expressions.example: The party was wonderful — we had a great time!
expressions.note: Une expression tres courante pour dire qu'on a apprecie un evenement ou une experience.

expressions.example: Lily blew out the candles and made a wish.
expressions.note: Dans la tradition anglaise, on fait un voeu avant de souffler les bougies d'anniversaire.

written.question: What present did they give to Lily?
written.explanation: The story says 'We gave Lily a present — a book about animals.'

written.question: Which is the correct past form of 'sing'?
written.explanation: 'Sing' is an irregular verb. The simple past is 'sang'. ('Sung' is the past participle, used with 'have'.)

written.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: She didn't told us her wish.
She didn't tell us her wish.
She not told us her wish.
She didn't telled us her wish.
written.explanation: In negative past sentences, use 'didn't + base form': didn't tell (NOT didn't told).

written.hint: The past tense of 'come' is irregular.

written.sentence: We ________ (give) Lily a present for her birthday.
written.hint: The past tense of 'give' is irregular.

written.sentence: She ________ (have) a big party at her house.
written.hint: The past tense of 'have' is irregular.

written.french: Nous avons danse et joue a des jeux dans le jardin.
written.suggested_answer: We danced and played games in the garden.

written.french: Elle n'a pas dit son voeu. Nous avons passe un super moment !
written.suggested_answer: She didn't tell us her wish. We had a great time!

oral.instruction: Read these sentences aloud. Pay attention to the irregular past tense verbs.
oral.text: Last Saturday, I went to Lily's birthday party. We gave her a present and she was very happy. Tom sang 'Happy Birthday' and we all danced. We had a great time!
oral.focus: Focus on pronouncing the irregular verbs clearly: went /wɛnt/, gave /ɡeɪv/, sang /sæŋ/, had /hæd/.

oral.question: Tell me about a birthday party you went to. What happened? What did you eat?
oral.suggested_answer: Last month, I went to my friend's birthday party. There were balloons and a big cake. We sang 'Happy Birthday' and danced. I gave my friend a book. We had a great time!
